meta.title: Double-charge investigation
meta.task: Investigate duplicate payment charge on order #4471

case_rounds.action: read code: retry wrapper + charge() implementation
case_rounds.outcome: suspected race condition (H1 open); no idempotency key found

case_rounds.action: pulled webhook + payment provider logs for order #4471
case_rounds.outcome: dismissed duplicate-webhook theory (H2); confirmed missing idempotency check as root cause (H3)

nodes.label: Retry Wrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps charge() calls with retry-on-timeout logic.

nodes.label: charge()
nodes.summary: Executes a payment charge against the provider.

nodes.label: Race condition in payment retry
nodes.evidence: retry() wraps charge() without an idempotency key — src/payment/retry.js:18-24; needs a concurrency repro to confirm shape/incidence split.

nodes.label: Duplicate webhook delivery
nodes.summary: Webhook provider dedupe key observed present in logs; single actual delivery confirmed for order #4471.
nodes.evidence: dismissed: webhook logs show a dedupe-key header on both attempts, provider confirms only one actual delivery (ops log, 2026-07-14).

nodes.label: Missing idempotency check in charge()
nodes.summary:
shape: confirmed — src/payment/charge.js:42
incidence: observed duplicate POST /payments/charge in prod log at 2026-07-14T10:02:03Z (order #4471, two charge.js:42 calls 340ms apart)
nodes.evidence: prod log: two charge.js:42 invocations for order #4471, 340ms apart, both returning success.

edges.label: no idempotency key found
edges.evidence: retry.js:18-24 calls charge() with no dedupe/idempotency key

edges.label: missing check at charge.js:42
edges.evidence: charge.js:42 — no idempotency guard before provider call

edges.label: webhook dedupe key confirmed single delivery
edges.evidence: webhook logs: dedupe-key header present, provider confirms one delivery

flows.title: Double charge under retry race (reproduction)
flows.description: Retry wrapper re-invokes charge() before the first attempt returns; with no idempotency key both attempts succeed and the customer is charged twice.

steps.explanation: Timeout fires before attempt 1 returns; retry logic calls charge() again — no idempotency key means the second call is not deduped
Both attempts complete successfully — the customer is charged twice for order #4471

advisory.summary: Verdict: charge() (src/payment/charge.js:42) has no idempotency check, so the retry wrapper's second attempt after a timeout is not deduped — both attempts succeed and order #4471 is charged twice. The duplicate-webhook theory (H2) is dismissed (provider dedupe key confirms a single delivery); the race-condition framing (H1) is subsumed by the confirmed missing-check root cause (H3). Recommended fix: add an idempotency key to the charge() call path.

steps.md: Round 1 suspected a **race condition** in the retry wrapper — the code lacked an idempotency key (*incidence: unverified — static shape only*).
Round 2 checked webhook delivery logs and **dismissed** the duplicate-webhook theory: the provider's dedupe key confirmed only one actual delivery.
**Confirmed**: `charge()` at `src/payment/charge.js:42` has no idempotency check. Production logs show two charge attempts 340ms apart for order #4471.
